channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in Holiday Shopping: The Future is Now
item.description: How AI-powered shopping assistants, search engines, and virtual try-on tools are revolutionizing the holiday shopping experience
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Healthcare Startup Suki Aims to Give Clinicians More Time Back with AI Platform Partnership with Google Cloud
item.description: Healthcare startup Suki partners with Google Cloud, aiming to give clinicians more time back via its AI platform that generates notes and selects billing codes.
item.dc:creator: Shalene Gupta, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s Grok-2 is now free—and it’s a mess
item.description: Late Friday night, Elon Musk’s xAI opened up Grok-2 access to the masses. The model isn’t all that different from the original Grok, though the company claims it runs faster and has improved accuracy. And yet, X users have spent the weekend dunking on it.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Targeting citizens based on their political views is illegal, said EU data watchdog
item.description: The EU Commission was found to illegally target citizens with ads using sensitive personal data based on their political views, the European Data Protection Supervisor's decision can reveal.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Salesforce reveals major hiring push to sell AI products
item.description: 2,000 sales representative jobs have come up at Salesforce as it prepares to handle increased demand for its AI tools.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fake AI Clips of Celebrities on the Rise: YouTube and CAA Join Forces
item.description: YouTube and Creative Artists Agency (CAA) collaborate to detect and remove fake AI clips of celebrities, protecting artists' rights and preventing identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk's favorability similar to Trump's: Survey
item.description: American's have a similar view of President-elect Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk — a key ally as he prepares to return to the White House — when it comes to favorability, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: This viral thread about AirPods Pro 2 as hearing aids shows how good they are –and why Apple should take a lesson from its own Beats division
item.description: Hearing aid mode is excellent, but a few cosmetic options would be nice – and it's an issue Beats already fixed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The OnePlus 13 and 13R could be the first real Samsung Galaxy S25 competitors
item.description: OnePlus has announced a January 7 release date for its latest flagship, the OnePlus 13, as well as its midrange companion, the OnePlus 13R.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How resilient CIOs future-proof to mitigate risks
item.description: This year saw emerging risks posed by AI, disastrous outages like the CrowdStrike incident, and surmounting software supply chain frailties, as well as the risk of cyberattacks and quantum computing breaking today’s most advanced encryption algorithms.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kazakhstan's Revolutionary "Carpet CCTV" Project: A New Era in Public Safety
item.description: Kazakhstan's Ministry of Internal Affairs has launched a groundbreaking surveillance project, combining AI and analytics to prevent crime and improve public safety.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The latest twist in the embarrassing WordPress saga involves pineapple pizza
item.description: The drama at WordPress has taken a number of twists and turns in the months since Matt Mullenweg, founder and CEO of for-profit WordPress host Automattic, shut off access to WP Engine, a third-party platform that many users deploy to host their websites.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: JetBrains launches search portal for Kotlin Multiplatform libraries
item.description: JetBrains has introduced klibs.io, a web service intended to make it easier to find libraries for Kotlin Multiplatform, a project that allows developers to build cross-platform mobile, desktop, and web apps from the same code base.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools
item.description: EPA's CIO says modernized tools and integrated teams help the agency meet the changing needs of the public more easily and consistently. The post EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon investing another $10 billion in Ohio-based data centers
item.description: Amazon Web Services will invest another $10 billion to bolster its data center infrastructure in Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 19 percent in new survey have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency
item.description: About 1-in-5 voters have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency, according to a Tuesday poll. The survey from Emerson College Polling found that 19 percent of respondents have traded, invested or used crypto like Bitcoin or Ethereum. About 81 percent have not.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5 reasons why 2025 will be the year of OpenTelemetry
item.description: Some open-source projects are spectacularly successful and become standard components of the IT infrastructure. Take Linux or Kubernetes, for example.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 6 sneaky scams that could ruin your holiday season
item.description: Beware of these six sneaky holiday scams. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives you tips to avoid falling victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 12 easy, techy ways to make the holiday smoother
item.description: To make the busiest time of year more manageable, here are some tricks for tracking your packages, taking quality family photos and curating the perfect Christmas playlist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft: No support or updates for Windows 11 PCs without minimum hardware requirements
item.description: Microsoft has offered a miniscule concession to users determined to install Windows 11 on PCs that don’t meet its minimum hardware requirements: you will be able to do it, but on your own head be it should things go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AMD data center chips vulnerable to revealing data through ‘BadRAM’ attack
item.description: AMD’s Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV), meant to protect processor memory from prying eyes in virtual machine (VM) environments, can be tricked into giving access to its encrypted memory contents using a test rig costing less than $10, researchers have revealed.
item.dc:creator: csoonline
